title: Fix /api/v1/overflows hang and slow fallback
description:
Two related fixes after troubleshooting blank map on phone:

1. Backend latestReadings() in db/.../ReadingRepository.kt has no polled_at lower bound, causing PG to walk every readings partition for each site in the bbox. Endpoint hangs >30s on any non-empty bbox; empty bbox returns in 87ms. Adding 'AND r.polled_at >= now() - interval 6 hours' prunes to recent partition.

2. HybridOverflowRepository in shared/.../HybridOverflowRepository.kt waits the full 30s Ktor request timeout before falling back to ArcGIS, leaving the map blank for 30s when backend misbehaves. Per-request timeout of 3s on the backend call makes the fallback near-instant on failure.
close_reason: Both fixes committed (313c9a7); shared+db tests green

title: Cache national worst offenders with 6-hour refresh
description: Pre-compute the national top 10 worst offenders every 6 hours instead of querying on each request. Store in memory, serve instantly. Reduces load on NAS and speeds up the National tab.
close_reason: Implemented - 6-hour cache with AtomicReference, instant endpoint response

title: Show 'Last discharged X ago' on info card
description: When user taps a pin, fetch site history from backend and show 'Last discharge: X ago' or 'Never recorded discharging' on the info card. Uses GET /api/v1/sites/{company}/{siteId}/history endpoint.
close_reason: Implemented - shows last discharge time on info card when pin tapped

title: Dynamic viewport fetching with debounce
description: Fetch overflow data for the visible map region as the user pans and zooms, replacing the fixed user-location radius approach. Includes debounce (500ms), minimum interval (5s), in-flight cancellation, and yellow pins for recently-stopped discharges.

title: Replace list UI with full-screen map view
description: Replace the current list-based overflow display with a full-screen interactive map using Google Maps (Android) and MapKit (iOS) via expect/actual pattern. Includes coloured pins (red=discharging, green=ok, grey=offline), user location marker, floating info cards on pin tap, summary chip, refresh FAB, directions button to native maps app, and hybrid proximity filtering (1mi radius OR nearest 5). See docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-12-map-view-design.md for full spec.
close_reason: Map view feature complete — Android + iOS tested on device/simulator

title: Proximity filter - overflows within 1 mile
description: Given user lat/lng and list of OverflowPoints, filter to those within 1 mile using Haversine formula. Pure Kotlin in shared module, no platform dependencies.
close_reason: Extension function withinRadius() on List<OverflowPoint> using Haversine, sorted by distance

title: GPS location service with expect/actual
description: Platform-specific location service: Android uses FusedLocationProviderClient, iOS uses CLLocationManager. Expose via expect/actual pattern. Request location permissions and return current lat/lng.
close_reason: LocationProvider interface with Android (LocationManager) and iOS (CLLocationManager) implementations

title: Build shared API client for water company overflow data
description: Ktor-based HTTP client in shared KMP module that queries all 10 water company endpoints, normalises responses into a common OverflowPoint data class (id, lat, lng, status, watercourse, statusStart). Handle pagination for ArcGIS endpoints.
close_reason: Ktor client with 3 adapter types covering all 10 water companies, OSGB converter, parallel fetch via OverflowRepository

title: Discover and document all water company API endpoints
description: Examine POOPy source code to extract exact ArcGIS FeatureServer URLs and field mappings for all 10 water companies. Document the normalised data model we need. Thames Water uses a custom REST API, the rest use ArcGIS.
close_reason: All 10 water company API endpoints documented with field mappings, coord systems, status values

title: Scaffold KMP project with Compose Multiplatform
description: Set up Kotlin Multiplatform project using the official KMP wizard template. Shared module for business logic, composeApp for UI, target Android + iOS. Include Ktor client and kotlinx.serialization dependencies.
close_reason: KMP project scaffolded with Compose Multiplatform, Ktor, kotlinx.serialization

title: Phase A: Client-only overflow map
description: Cross-platform mobile app (KMP + Compose Multiplatform) that shows storm overflow discharge status within 1 mile of user's GPS location. No backend - app polls water company APIs directly.

title: Backend test compile error: WorstOffendersRoutesTest missing nationalRankingCache arg
description: WorstOffendersRoutesTest.kt:39 calls worstOffendersRoutes(database, siteRepo, readingRepo) but the route function now requires a 4th nationalRankingCache parameter (added in commit 2594772 'Cache national worst offenders with 6-hour refresh'). The test wasn't updated and won't compile, so :backend:test is currently broken on main. Discovered while running tests during work on turd-alert-v5p.
close_reason: Plumbed NationalRankingCache through test setup; :backend:test now passes

title: Persist national ranking cache to Postgres table
description: Write national worst offenders to a rankings table with a computed_at timestamp. On startup, serve from the most recent row immediately while recomputing in the background. Keep all historical rows so we can show how rankings change over time in the future.

title: Fix event count in national worst offenders
description: The national endpoint hardcodes eventCount to 0. Need a window function or post-processing to count discharge start transitions (status goes from 0 to 1). The local worst-offenders endpoint calculates this correctly via siteHistory + statsResponse.

title: Wall of Shame - top 10 worst offenders nationwide
description: New backend endpoint GET /api/v1/sites/worst-offenders/national?days=30 that ranks all sites by total discharge hours without spatial filter. New app screen accessible from top bar showing the 10 worst CSOs in the country.
close_reason: Implemented - Near Me / National tabs with SQL-ranked top offenders

title: Worst offenders near me screen
description: List screen showing top 20 worst sites by discharge hours near user location. Uses GET /api/v1/sites/worst-offenders endpoint.
close_reason: Implemented - ranked list screen accessible from top bar icon

title: Discharge timeline chart on info card
description: Horizontal bar chart on the info card showing last 7/30 days of discharge history. Red blocks = discharging, gaps = clean. Uses history endpoint timeline data.

title: Site discharge stats on info card
description: Show total discharge hours, event count, % of time discharging on the info card. Uses history endpoint stats data.
close_reason: Implemented - shows event count, total hours, and percentage on info card

title: Historical overflow data: polling service, storage, and visualization
description:
Long-running service that polls all 10 water company APIs hourly to build point-in-time readings for every sensor. Includes three sub-systems:

1. **Polling service** — long-running process fetching all APIs on a schedule (hourly). Needs to handle ~14,187 overflow points across 10 APIs.
2. **Storage layer** — persist historical time-series data. Need to decide on storage tech (SQLite/Postgres/time-series DB) and schema.
3. **Data visualization** — compelling way to show historical discharge trends in the app.

Key decisions to make:
- Runtime environment for the poller (JVM app on VPS, Docker container, serverless cron, etc.)
- Storage technology and schema design
- How to surface historical data in the mobile app (charts, timelines, heatmaps, etc.)

This aligns with Phase B from the project roadmap. Each sub-system should be broken into its own task once we brainstorm the approach.

title: Map UI with overflow markers
description: Compose Multiplatform screen showing a map centred on user location with markers for nearby overflows. Colour-coded: red=discharging, green=not discharging, grey=offline. Tap marker for details (watercourse name, duration). Pull-to-refresh.
close_reason: List-based UI with ViewModel, OverflowCard (status dots, duration), pull-to-refresh, error/loading/no-location states

title: Company discharge league table
description: Aggregation view showing total discharge hours per water company. Needs new backend endpoint for company-level aggregation.

title: Rain correlation overlay
description: Overlay Met Office rainfall data against discharge events to show correlation. Needs new data source integration.
